A life without glizzy is one not worth living. I find myself in circumstances in which there is above all else a supreme lack of glizzy. I am beginning to forget the feeling of the glizzy crushing soft and luscious between by teeth, the condiments sliding out and saturating the awaiting taste buds nearby. The glizzy is not just a food. It is a lifestyle to follow, a religion to pray to, and a pillar of society to go to when all else fails.
